title: Shattering Quick-Fix Earthly Polish Dish
description: A comforting, earthy Polish dish that's easy to make and shatters with a satisfying crunch. Made with ground allspice and a deglaze for added depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Ground Allspice
ingredients.notes: Fodmap-friendly alternative to traditional Polish dishes.

ingredients.name: Deglaze (Apple Cider Vinegar)
ingredients.notes: Adds depth of flavor and a tangy twist.

ingredients.name: Potato
ingredients.notes: For shattering, use waxy potatoes like Yukon Gold.

instructions: In a large skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add ground pork and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add grated carrot, ground allspice, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Deglaze with apple cider vinegar, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Add butter and stir until melted.
Pour mixture into a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Arrange sliced potatoes on top, slightly overlapping each other.
Dot top with butter and season with salt and black pepper.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until potatoes are tender and golden brown.
Remove from oven and let cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh parsley, if desired.
Slice into portions and serve hot, shattering with a satisfying crunch.
Serve with a side of traditional Polish rye bread or a green salad for a well-rounded meal.
This dish is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner or a special occasion meal.
It's also a great option for a gluten-free or fodmap-friendly meal plan.
To make ahead, assemble the dish up to the baking step and refrigerate or freeze until ready to bake.
When ready to serve, bake as instructed and let cool slightly before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
